design.title: SAR ADC Indexed Design Reference
design.headline: Reusable SAR ADC architecture, verification contract, and results wrapper.
design.summary: Indexed reference for a SAR ADC top-level design so imported converter netlists can participate in the same architecture and results flow as other AMS designs.
design.narrative: This manifest captures the reusable architecture intent for a top-level successive-approximation ADC. It preserves the design partition, the main sizing equations, the transient-oriented validation flow, and the file locations needed to regenerate the design-reference page and portfolio presentation.
design.technology: generic180 baseline with portability to generic130, generic65, and bcd180 through TechMapper wrappers
design.signoff_boundary: Current validation is a reusable top-level snapshot on an imported reference design. Replace with block-level transistor implementations and foundry signoff flows for silicon work.

flow_summary: The SAR ADC follows the same indexed flow as the ASIC designs: capture architecture, define verification settings, run a reproducible snapshot, and publish the result in HTML and portfolio outputs.
analysis_summary: Primary validation is transient-oriented because the converter behavior depends on sampled timing, internal trial voltages, and conversion sequencing rather than a single DC operating point.
script_summary: Use the snapshot script to turn a block spec into a normalized regression artifact, then render the HTML page or portfolio PPT from the saved results.

architecture.summary: Analog input is sampled, compared against a DAC trial voltage, and resolved bit by bit by SAR logic until the digital code converges. The indexed wrapper keeps this architecture reusable even when the underlying implementation file changes.
architecture.power_tree_summary: 3.3V supply and 2.5V reference feed the sampled-data conversion path.

domains.name: VDD
domains.drives: sampling path, comparator model, SAR logic model

power_sequence: Establish VREF before conversion starts so the DAC trial range is well-defined.
Sample the analog input and isolate it on the hold capacitor before bit cycling.
Run the SAR loop from MSB to LSB until the code converges.

control_path.title: Sample phase
control_path.description: The front-end captures the analog input on the hold node.

control_path.title: Bit trial phase
control_path.description: SAR logic drives DAC trial codes and the comparator decides whether to keep each bit.

control_path.title: Code output phase
control_path.description: The final digital code represents the closest available quantized value.

indexed_flow.title: Capture converter architecture
indexed_flow.description: Freeze the sample/hold, comparator, DAC, and SAR control partition before discussing timing or accuracy.

indexed_flow.title: Import or generate implementation
indexed_flow.description: Wrap the chosen converter source file into the indexed design flow so reporting and reuse do not depend on where the implementation originated.

indexed_flow.title: Run the top-level snapshot
indexed_flow.description: Execute the transient snapshot and save a normalized regression JSON for presentation and review.

indexed_flow.title: Publish reusable reference
indexed_flow.description: Generate the standalone HTML page and fold the result into the portfolio presentation.

analysis_playbook.name: Top-level conversion snapshot
analysis_playbook.purpose: Capture the analog input waveform and internal trial nodes during conversion.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: Transient results include the analog input and at least one internal conversion node with sufficient time samples.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: Source waveform, internal DAC node, hold node, and comparator activity.

analysis_playbook.name: Portfolio publication
analysis_playbook.purpose: Surface the converter in the cross-design architecture and results presentation.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: The design appears in JSON, Markdown, and PPT outputs with its latest status.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: Manifest completeness and saved regression artifact naming.

corner_closure.summary: No PVT closure is claimed yet. This wrapper only records the intended structure for future technology-specific converter work.

matrix.process: generic180 baseline today; future TT/FF/SS and foundry corners

closure_order: Close transient conversion behavior before claiming any dynamic accuracy metric.
Add comparator, DAC, and logic sub-block verification before PVT closure.
Promote the imported reference into a technology-specific implementation only after architecture is stable.
corner_closure.pass_rule: Current pass means the imported top-level source executed with the required visibility for architecture review. It does not mean silicon signoff closure.
corner_closure.model_note: Technology portability is architecture-driven today. Swap in PDK blocks and extracted models for real converter implementation work.

script_index.name: Run top-level snapshot
script_index.purpose: Create a normalized regression JSON from spec.yaml verification settings.

script_index.name: Generate SAR ADC webpage
script_index.purpose: Render the indexed SAR ADC reference as a standalone HTML page.

script_index.name: Generate portfolio PPT
script_index.purpose: Include the SAR ADC in the overall architecture and results presentation.

debug_playbook.title: If no internal nodes appear
debug_playbook.description: Check whether the imported source still exposes the DAC, hold, or comparator nodes expected by the snapshot checks.

debug_playbook.title: If transient coverage is too short
debug_playbook.description: Increase tstop or reduce tstep until one full conversion window is visible.

future_design_reuse.title: Swap in a different SAR implementation
future_design_reuse.description: Only the source path and verification settings need to change when retargeting the wrapper to another SAR ADC design.

future_design_reuse.title: Retarget technology
future_design_reuse.description: Keep the indexed architecture and update the implementation source plus technology metadata for the next node.

blocks.name: SAR ADC Top Level
blocks.role: Converts an analog input into a digital code using sampled-data comparison and successive approximation.
blocks.architecture: The imported top-level reference models the sample-and-hold, trial DAC, comparator, and SAR decision flow in one reusable netlist.

implementation_steps.title: Capture the analog sample
implementation_steps.description: The analog input is sampled onto the hold node before bit trials begin.

implementation_steps.title: Drive trial DAC codes
implementation_steps.description: The internal DAC reconstructs the tentative analog voltage for each SAR bit decision.

implementation_steps.title: Resolve the code
implementation_steps.description: The comparator and SAR logic keep or clear each bit until the final code is reached.

primary_specs.name: Resolution
primary_specs.reason: Defines quantization depth and code space.

sizing_formulas.title: LSB size
sizing_formulas.explanation: Defines the quantization step for an N-bit SAR ADC.

sizing_formulas.title: Conversion time
sizing_formulas.explanation: A simple SAR approximation: one major decision per bit clock.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Input source inside the imported reference netlist

dc_operating_points.target: Tracks trial code against VREF range
dc_operating_points.how_set: Internal DAC reconstruction from SAR bit decisions
dc_operating_points.why: Comparator reference for each bit trial.
Keeps the analog sample stable during bit cycling.

analysis_steps.name: Top-level converter snapshot
analysis_steps.purpose: Observe input sampling and internal SAR node activity over a conversion window.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Input waveform, internal conversion nodes, and sufficient time samples are all present.

corner_focus.risk: Comparator delay, DAC settling, and logic timing can all shift differently across technologies.
corner_focus.primary_knob: Technology-specific sub-block implementation and timing budgets

tuning_knobs.title: VREF
tuning_knobs.description: Moves full-scale range and the effective LSB size.

tuning_knobs.title: Clock period
tuning_knobs.description: Controls the available settling and decision time per bit.

files.role: Top-level verification contract for the SAR ADC wrapper
